evidences.title: Building an Asynchronous OHLCV Data Downloader for Binance ...
evidences.content: creates concurrent tasks to fetch OHLCV data for each symbol in the provided list, over the specified time range and interval. Parameters: ----------- symbols : List[str] A list of trading pair symbols (e.g., ['BTCUSDT', 'ETHUSDT']). interval : str The candlestick interval (e.g., '1m', '1h', '1d'). start : int The start time in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. end : int The end time in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. Returns: -------- Dict[str, List[List[Any]]] A dictionary where each key is a symbol and the corresponding value is a list of OHLCV data points. Each data point is a list containing [timestamp, open, high, low, close, [...] ``` async def main(): client = BinanceClient() symbols = ["BTCUSDT", "ETHUSDT", "BNBUSDT", "XRPUSDT", "XLMUSDT"] interval = "15m" start = int(time() 1000) - 5 365 24 60 60 1000 # 5 years ago end = int(time() 1000) st = time() data = await client.download_ohlcv_data(symbols, interval, start, end) et = int((time() - st) 1000) no_of_klines = sum(len(v) for v in data.values()) print(f"got {no_of_klines} klines in {et}ms") for symbol, ohlcv in data.items(): print(f"{symbol}: {len(ohlcv)} candles downloaded")if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(...

evidences.title: General API Information | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: With both, behaves like `startTime` but does not exceed `endTime`. [...] All time and timestamp related fields in the JSON responses are in milliseconds by default. To receive the information in microseconds, please add the header `X-MBX-TIME-UNIT:MICROSECOND` or `X-MBX-TIME-UNIT:microsecond`. We support HMAC, RSA, and Ed25519 keys. For more information, please see API Key types. Timestamp parameters (e.g. `startTime`, `endTime`, `timestamp`) can be passed in milliseconds or microseconds. For APIs that only send public market data, please use the base endpoint Please refer to Market Data Only page. If there are enums or terms you want clarification on, please see the SPOT Glossary for more information. [...] should give better performance but have less stability. Responses are in JSON by default. To receive responses in SBE, refer to the SBE FAQ page. If your request contains a symbol name containing non-ASCII characters, then the response may contain non-ASCII characters encoded in UTF-8. Some endpoints may return asset and/or symbol names containing non-ASCII characters encoded in UTF-8 even if the request did not contain non-ASCII characters. Data is returned in chronological...

evidences.title: Multi-Timeframe ETH/USDT OHLCV Data (2019–2024) - Kaggle
evidences.content: This dataset solves all of that by: Providing 12 distinct timeframes (1m to 1d) Ensuring clean, sorted, and complete records Using a standard OHLCV schema that aligns with trading systems and model inputs Covering an extensive historical range, long enough for statistically valid backtests and long-term model training Being stored in easy-to-read Excel files, compatible with Python, R, Excel, Power BI, etc. 🔍 Data Breakdown Each record (row) represents one candlestick (also called a "kline") for a specific time interval. Columns (Features): Column Description Timestamp Start time of the candlestick interval (UTC). It's the reference time for the open of that candle. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. Open ETH/USDT price at the start of the interval (i.e., opening price). [...] High Maximum price reached by ETH during the interval. Low Minimum price reached by ETH during the interval. Close ETH/USDT price at the end of the interval (i.e., closing price). Volume The total volume of ETH traded during the interval (base asset volume, not USDT). All values are as recorded from the Binance exchange without transformation, preserving their integrity for accurate modeling and testing. 🗃️ Time...

evidences.title: ETH may pull back to $2500 in the short term, but it will aim ...
evidences.content: Key conclusion: - As market capitalization expands, the rate of increase decreases each round, but each round still maintains several times the market. - If we take the current low of $2,500 as a benchmark, 3 times that would be $7,500, and 4 times that would be $10,000, which is highly consistent with the $7,000-$9,000 range. Second, the current pullback is only 36%, far from entering the "deep bear market" range. As of November 26, 2025, the price of ETH was around $3,000, a pullback of about 36% from its all-time high of $4,909. Compare with historical drawdowns: - Drawdown after the 2018 bull market: -94% - Post-2022 bull market pullback: -82% - Current pullback: -36%, still within the normal correction range and has not yet entered the oversold phase. evidences.title: Ethereum Price Prediction 2026-2031
evidences.content: UP_WALA_TRADER1 a day ago $ETH Analysis: Dip Before Bounce or More Downside Ahead? Understanding the Chart (Simple View): Looking at ETH on the 15-minute chart, we can see the price was moving up slowly, forming higher candles. But after reaching near the $2,170 area, the momentum changed. Sellers stepped in, and the candles started dropping quickly. Right now, price is sitting around $2,130, trying to hold steady after the fall. What’s Happening Now: It looks like a short-term bearish trend because of the sharp red candles. The market lost strength after the recent high, and buyers are now trying to defend the current level. Key Levels to Watch: Support: Around $2,120 – $2,130 → Price is trying to hold here Resistance: Around $2,160 – $2,170 → Strong rejection zone earlier [...] A green candle indicates that ETH closed higher than it opened during a specific timeframe, signaling possible upward momentum. Conversely, a red candle means the closing price was lower, reflecting the possibility of selling pressure. In addition, it is important to watch whether candles closed with a big wick or body, as it can show whether momentum on ETH is strong or starting to weaken. ### Factors...

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to ge...

evidences.title: EST to UTC - Online Converter
evidences.content: OnlineConverter.com # EST to UTC Eastern Standard Time (EST) Date Time ## Calculation Results | | | --- | | Time Zone | Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) | | Time | ## Introduction This free tool can help you convert Eastern Standard Time (EST) to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). You just need to select the data and time of the source time zone and the tool will calculate the date and time for the destination time zone. ## How To Use Select a date by using the "Date" option. Select a time by using the "Time" option. Click button "Convert" to calculate. The calculation results will be displayed in table. ## Remarks [...] Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UT...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Prediction 2026-2031
evidences.content: $BTC View More Image 19 0 2 0 0 Image 20: UP_WALA_TRADER1Image 21: UP_WALA_TRADER1 UP_WALA_TRADER1 19 hours ago $BTC USDT Analysis: Pullback or Start of a Bigger Drop? Understanding the Chart (Simple View): Looking at the 15-minute chart, Bitcoin recently pushed up strongly toward the $71,200–$71,400 zone, but then things changed. The price started to struggle, forming smaller candles at the top — a sign buyers were losing strength. After that, we saw a sharp drop with multiple red candles, showing strong selling pressure. What’s Happening Now: Right now, price has fallen near the $70,000 area and is trying to bounce slightly. This looks like a short-term reaction after a fast drop, not a strong recovery yet. Key Levels to Watch: [...] A green candle indicates that BTC closed higher than it opened during a specific timeframe, signaling possible upward momentum. Conversely, a red candle means the closing price was lower, reflecting the possibility of selling pressure. In addition, it is important to watch whether candles closed with a big wick or body, as it can show whether momentum on BTC is strong or starting to weaken. ### Factors That May Affect Bitcoin Price Predictions [.....
